LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GRANITE CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S JOINDER TO MOTION TO STAY ACTION CONTINUED FROM JUL-12-2007 TO LAW AND MOTION AT AUG-02-2007, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO AUGUST 2, 2007, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PEAK ENGINEERING, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION PENDING RESOLUTION OF UNDERLYING THIRD PARTY LAWSUIT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO STAY DISCOVERY: MOTION TO STAY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TAKES INCONSISTENT POSITIONS. ON ONE HAND, THIS ACTION IS BASED SIMPLY ON THE "ALLEGATIONS" IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AND ON THE OTHER HAND, PLAINTIFF ASSERTS THE STAY WILL PRECLUDE "DISCOVERY TO VERIFY AND DOCUMENT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES". THE LATTER POSITION DEMONSTRATES THE OVERLAP BETWEEN THIS CASE AND THE UNDERLYING CASE. THROUGH ITS POSITION IN THE UNDERLYING CASE, PLAINTIFF WILL BE ABLE TO "VERIFY AND DOCUMENT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES" AND WILL SUFFER NO ADVERSE CONSEQUENCE AS A RESULT OF THE STAY. THE ARBITRATION WILL CLARIFY THE ACTUAL DAMAGES AND THE CAUSES THEREOF AND WILL PROVIDE A BASIS FOR DETERMINING COVERAGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

APPEARANCES: LALEAQUE GRAD, ESQUIRE (GRECO & TRAFICANTE, 619.234.3660, EXT. 217) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MICHAEL MAZZOCONE, ESQUIRE (415.399.0800) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT PEAK ENGINEERING.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TAKES INCONSISTENT POSITIONS. ON ONE HAND, THIS ACTION IS BASED SIMPLY ON THE "ALLEGATIONS" IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AND ON THE OTHER HAND, PLAINTIFF ASSERTS THE STAY WILL PRECLUDE "DISCOVERY TO VERIFY AND DOCUMENT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES". THE LATTER POSITION DEMONSTRATES THE OVERLAP BETWEEN THIS CASE AND THE UNDERLYING CASE. THROUGH ITS POSITION IN THE UNDERLYING CASE, PLAINTIFF WILL BE ABLE TO "VERIFY AND DOCUMENT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES" AND WILL SUFFER NO ADVERSE CONSEQUENCE AS A RESULT OF THE STAY. THE ARBITRATION WILL CLARIFY THE ACTUAL DAMAGES AND THE CAUSES THEREOF AND WILL PROVIDE A BASIS FOR DETERMINING COVERAGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J.
